tweets.text:
Yen shorts are back to their highest level since 2017, basically equivalent to positioning just before the 2024 carry unwind.

USDJPY is trading around the key 160 level with the ongoing BOJ meeting and Warsh's first FOMC press conference Wednesday.

I think Japan ultimately has to let 160 go given the depth of their fiscal problems, but I don't see why they would give record shorts a free ride on the way.
tweets.quoted_tweet_text: https://t.co/v2nhejY0zR

tweets.text:
Interesting piece, but I disagree. "Political Islam" will never really vanish in the Middle East, the legalistic tradition makes it a permanent feature. The question is whether Middle Eastern countries engage with modernity, aka the West, or not, this is what separates most states and most terrorist groups. That's also the question they've been wrestling with since the 19th century.  However, what may be dead is the revolutionary fever that Khomeini and other nationalists espoused. That distinction is fundamental.

https://t.co/p14nrGqXP3

tweets.text:
The resistance doctrine is losing its audience in the Middle East and finding a different kind of host in the West, among populations with their own grievances against the Western order, their own suspicions of American power, and institutions sufficiently open to make the resistance proposition feel like dissent. The next phase of this confrontation may unfold less in the streets of Tehran or Sanaa than in the parliamentary debates and cultural institutions of Europe, where the ideas that the Middle East is already moving past are still gaining ground. While that shift bodes ill for Europe and for the West, it nonetheless is a sign of resounding defeat for the ideology of the Islamic Revolution.
https://t.co/lnepHyMK4h

tweets.text:
markets are in general, optimistic and find a bull case no matter what is happening. The stock market has a 100% recovery rate

unemployment up is bullish bc fed will accomodate
unemployment down is bullish bc economy is strong 

there is always a bullish tilt on everything, good or bad

for most people, the only way to make it is capital markets, and many understand that now. this is the new norm now. You have to try to be optimistic 90% of the time, but the 10% you are pessimistic the timing has to be pretty damn good. 

in the end bulls always win in the long run. bc the only way to win is to have exposure and be in the game.

tweets.text:
i disagree

you’re not betting on millions of people buying an nft collection. i mean, there aren’t millions of pudgy penguins or millions of azukis

just like buying an original 1996 base set charizard card isn’t betting that millions of people will buy base set charizards

but if millions of people LIKE charizards, then there will be hundreds of rich dudes bidding the limited supply of OG ones

that’s the bet. you could argue that it’s a bad bet, or that the current odds are overvalued, but i think it’s important to state the bet correctly

tweets.text:
0.78% of launches raise a Series A.

Top factors that have impact on it ↓

1. How high are you on ProductHunt on the launch date
2. Number of upvotes & engagement (engagement is more important)
3. Founder's follower count
4. Votes-per-comment ratio (engaged discussions preferred)
5. B2B topic cluster

tweets.text:
Cross-chain trading still has too many trade-offs.

You either get speed on a CEX and give up custody, or stay onchain and deal with fragmented liquidity, slower execution, and worse routing.

@EuclidProtocol has been working on addressing these nuisances with their new Prime product. It's a cross-chain orderbook with:

→ Sub-50ms matching across 40+ networks
→ Orders matched offchain for faster execution
→ Onchain verification for deposits and withdrawals

The idea is pretty simple: make onchain trading feel less fragmented, without forcing traders back into the same custody trade-off they were trying to avoid.

Prime’s waitlist is now open before mainnet, with $100K+ in early rewards, including $50K USDC and an EUCLID token allocation revealed at launch.

Potential-wise, this gives me a similar early-window feeling to Ondo Perps. It's an opportunity to be early on a unique product and position ahead of the competition.

If interested, here's my ref link: https://t.co/4bJYSZHxF9
tweets.quoted_tweet_text:
Euclid Prime Waitlist is LIVE ✦
The orderbook without tradeoffs.

→ CEX-grade depth and execution
→ Without the custody risk or withdrawal delays
→ Full self-custody. Always.

Join the waitlist and climb it by referring friends. https://t.co/AzdcSm2wbT

→ Orders matched offchain for faster execution

The idea is pretty simple: make onchain trading feel less fragmented, without forcing traders back into the same custody trade-off they were trying to avoid.

Prime’s waitlist is now open before mainnet, with $100K+ in early rewards, including $50K USDC and an EUCLID token allocation revealed at launch.

Potential-wise, this gives me a similar early-window feeling to Ondo Perps. It's an opportunity to be early on a unique product and position ahead of the competition.

tweets.text:
Crypto's pretty quiet at the moment.

That's why I'm always searching for decent stablecoin yields to park my assets.

Right now, USD1 is paying better than any other stablecoin in the market.

Here are a few campaigns offering high yield across CEX and DeFi:

→ @Gate USD1 Soft Staking (up to 20% APR, adjusted daily)

→ USD1 @monad Vault by TownSquare × Native (16.92% APY + project points)

→ USD1 Lending on @Dolomite_io (~12% APR, ~26M USD1 borrowable left)

→ USD1 Vault by Lorenzo × Binance Wallet (~11% APY, runs until June 19)

→ Binance Simple Earn USD1 (~10.5% APR, tiered bonus)

→ @MEXC USD1 Stablecoin Earn (up to 9% APR)

Most are time-limited, so it's worth checking before they wind down.

Disclosure: I'm a USD1 holder.

tweets.text:
We’ve looked at almost every TCG platform in crypto. We passed on all of them except two.

@Collector_Crypt, when it was too niche for anyone else to touch.

And @Beezie, for reasons that won’t stay under the radar much longer.

If you had access to the same insights we do about what the stellar @AndreaMYellie and @Pratik_in_Web3 are cooking, the doubt would disappear instantly.

These two aren’t competitors in our book. They’re a barbell. Owning both means owning the entire category, vertically and horizontally.

In a few months this will all be obvious, when the long tail starts consolidating into these two winners. The smart collectors will be using Collector and Beezie, and nothing else.

Save yourself the regret and switch while you’re still early.

tweets.text: I don't think most politicians are *incapable* of understanding exponential growth. I think most could understand it if you gave them real world numbers like 15% per month growth and 5 years, and had them calculate 1.15^60 on their phones.

thread_tweets.text: I don't think most politicians are *incapable* of understanding exponential growth. I think most could understand it if you gave them real world numbers like 15% per month growth and 5 years, and had them calculate 1.15^60 on their phones.
thread_tweets.full_text: I don't think most politicians are *incapable* of understanding exponential growth. I think most could understand it if you gave them real world numbers like 15% per month growth and 5 years, and had them calculate 1.15^60 on their phones.

thread_tweets.text: Founders with technical backgrounds are often surprised by the results of such calculations too. Exponential growth is surprising. Indeed, that's probably as much as most people need to know about it: that it can produce crazy numbers.
thread_tweets.full_text: Founders with technical backgrounds are often surprised by the results of such calculations too. Exponential growth is surprising. Indeed, that's probably as much as most people need to know about it: that it can produce crazy numbers.

thread_tweets.text: And because exponential growth can produce crazy numbers, you need an extra dose of intellectual humility when dealing with it. It's never safe to make confident judgments like "You can't earn x dollars" when x is the result of exponential growth. X could be anything!
thread_tweets.full_text: And because exponential growth can produce crazy numbers, you need an extra dose of intellectual humility when dealing with it. It's never safe to make confident judgments like "You can't earn x dollars" when x is the result of exponential growth. X could be anything!

The Semi Annual Report was not removed from our website. Sounds like you have some difficulty navigating websites so let me help:

https://t.co/u24kZXilhi -> Public Filings -> Semi Annual Report

The report was initially uploaded to a cloud hosting provider as it wasnt on Edgar - then the reference was moved to Edgar once it was available there

NAV is a subjective mark. There can be lags between when new rounds occur and when NAV updates higher or lower. Theres a lot of nuance around NAV of private assets and what the public markets might value a portfolio at. 

Once you learn how to navigate a website we can discuss more

tweets.text:
Crypto's bleeding while everything else rips to highs.

I've been through several bear markets and this one's up there with the toughest.

One concept I like is bear market stamina - simply surviving and finding your own way to stay engaged until the market comes back. 

In past bear markets, I focused on messing around with new protocols and farming airdrops. 

This market I'm more focused around exploring AI. I stay engaged by using A.I. to help build new Crypto research workflows that make me way more efficient.

Here's some other ideas to help you through this downturn:

Imagine that you’re playing a game like bridge, poker, backgammon, or chess and have to make your move, and you have a computer that works with you to assess the circumstances and suggest a move. That’s what playing the investment game is like for me. 

I now want to share what the existing characteristics of the market look like to me and what I think should be done in light of them.

As always, I welcome your questions and thoughts.

tweets.text:
haven’t been back to Michigan since my high school graduation, now headed back for @reindsummit and having all the feels! 

i moved to America (from the Netherlands) as a kid in the mid 90s. i’ve been here for most of my life, 30 years come July, and am grateful every day to be living the American dream 🇺🇸

America is the greatest country on earth, full stop. there is a pervasive negativity about America and its future that i see on the timeline and it makes me sad. we cannot tolerate this version of our shared lore to spread. we must stamp it out with unbridled optimism and fearless action. 

for all its faults, America is the only place on earth with unlimited social and economic mobility for people who want to work hard and tackle ambitious problems, where people can raise billions of dollars to build insane things like fusion reactors and starships, and where failure is not only accepted but encouraged as part of the process of progress. 

we live in the most incredible time in human history, in an age of unprecedented abundance, where miracles happen every day. we must shake off this secular spiritual stagnation and embrace America’s awesomeness. yes we have problems *and* yes we can solve them. 

excited to hang with a bunch of badass humans this week and celebrate the potential to rebuild of America’s industrial base, from factory floor to frontier lab. 

long America forever 🫶

tweets.text: I bought $UBER June '26 puts in May '25, with Uber @ $87.50. Lots of people criticised me for it, because "Tesla needs Uber for Robotaxi" etc. Well, today Uber is $20 lower than it was when I bought the puts. Uber will keep going down, so I may roll the puts over to June 2027.

tweets.text:
Every generation of software made humans more productive. Excel made accountants more productive. CRM made salespeople more productive. ERP made operations more productive.

But all of it automated process, never judgment. The tacit knowledge of your best people (the pattern recognition, the contextual decisions, the expertise that made a twenty-year veteran irreplaceable) software could systematize around it but never encode it.

Token capital is the first system that crosses that line. It doesn't just enhance human judgment. It learns it, encodes it, and compounds it back into the organization with every decision made.

Token capital compounds human capital.

The sovereignty test is does your company's knowledge live inside the model (in which case the model provider owns it, not you) or outside it, in private evals, knowledge bases, organizational memory you control. The firms that win own everything below the model layer because it makes every future token cheaper and smarter than the last.

Firms won't own their own models. The capex is prohibitive and platforms like Microsoft will commoditize model access entirely. The real IP lives in the layer underneath.
tweets.quoted_tweet_text: https://t.co/vLmiBKTtX3

tweets.text:
Don't see these narratives emerging just yet....give it a week or two probably....

Underlying inflation may be slower than headline
“But if you look at shelter costs, which are the biggest component of CPI, I would say they’re running in the low 1s versus government data in the low 3s, and that will be helpful to the new Central Bank chair, particularly after the wars in the rearview mirror. And wages for us have gone from, call it, 5% in the U.S. 2 years ago to 3%. So again, that’s helpful. I think the picture after the war ends will be better than I think most people realize.” 

- Blackstone ($BX ) COO Jonathan Gray
